description: A nested dict literal used as a dict key (`print { { a: 1 }: 2 }`) is a grammar-position error, not a brace-matching one: dict-key ::= identifier | number (spec/grammar.md), so the inner `{` reports exactly one ol-bad-token — never ol-unmatched-brace — and the outer, correctly-matched dict literal still closes cleanly (spec/error-model.md, spec/data-structures.md#dictionaries, issue #520).
